emul_method: The organic phase, consisting of 2 mg of MF and 6 mg of PLGA in 1 mL of acetone, was rapidly poured into 2 mL of aqueous solution containing 0.1% (w/v) Solutol HS 15. The suspension was stirred at 900 rpm for 24 h to allow complete evaporation of the organic solvent, and the formulation volume was adjusted with water to 2 mL. Complete evaporation was confirmed by weighting the glass vial before addition of the organic phase and after the evaporation process. Then, the formulation was centrifuged for 1 min at 3000 rpm to discard debris. The supernatant was then transferred to a new tube for further investigation.
notes: Primary formulation before freeze-drying.

emul_method: The PLGA–PEG copolymer was synthesized as previously reported. All nanoparticles were prepared using nanoprecipitation. PLGA–PEG (50 mg) was dissolved in ACN (10 mL) and added to 0.2% w/v PVA in water (100 mL) at 0.5 mL/min. Kartogenin (5 mg) was included in the polymer solution prior to precipitation. This solution was stirred at 60 °C for 4 h before collecting the nanoparticles by centrifugation [15,000 rpm, 20 min, room temperature (RT, 20 °C)] and washing three times with water.
notes: 50 mg of PLGA-PEG copolymer was used for nanoprecipitation. The specific mass of the PLGA component within the copolymer is not stated.

emul_method: The PLGA–PEG copolymer was synthesized as previously reported. All nanoparticles were prepared using nanoprecipitation. PLGA–PEG (50 mg) was dissolved in ACN (10 mL) and added to 0.2% w/v PVA in water (100 mL) at 0.5 mL/min. Kartogenin (5 mg) was included in the polymer solution prior to precipitation. This solution was stirred at 60 °C for 4 h before collecting the nanoparticles by centrifugation [15,000 rpm, 20 min, room temperature (RT, 20 °C)] and washing three times with water. For PLGA–PEG–HA nanoparticles, HA was de-salted by dialyzing against 0.1 M HCl. De-salted HA was solubilized in 0.01 M MES buffer (5 mg/mL, pH 5.5) and activated using EDC and sulfo-NHS (1:5:5 molar ratio) for 2 h at RT under N2. Blank or KGN-loaded PLGA–PEG nanoparticles in MES buffer were added to the activated HA solution (3:1 w/w HA:nanoparticle), and stirred for 24 h at RT under N2. The nanoparticles were collected by centrifugation (15,000 rpm, 20 min, RT) and washed three times.
notes: PLGA-PEG-HA nanoparticles were formed by conjugating HA to pre-formed PLGA-PEG nanoparticles. The PLGA-PEG core was initially made using 50 mg of PLGA-PEG copolymer. The specific mass of the PLGA component within the copolymer is not stated. HA molecular weight was 39 kDa. PEG-bis-amine molecular weight was 2 kDa.

NPs were successfully developed by nanoprecipitation method using PLGA as polymer, poloxamer as surfactant and acetone as

emul_method: Injection of organic phase (polymer + drug in acetone) into aqueous phase (double-distilled water, pH 7.0) under magnetic stirring (500 rpm) via syringe pump (10 mL/min), followed by solvent removal under reduced pressure for 3 hours.
notes: Polymer used was VS(72)-10, a biocompatible, branched polyester: poly(vinyl sulfonate-co-vinyl alcohol)-graft-poly(D,L-lactide-co-glycolide). The '72' refers to the total average number of sulfonate functions on the PVA backbone, and '10' to the PLGA side-chain length. Increased negative zeta potential was observed compared to PLGA nanoparticles due to negatively charged functional groups in the polymer structure, promoting electrostatic interactions with salbutamol. All characterization values (size, PDI, zeta-potential, encapsulation efficiency, drug loading) are mean values from Table 1, measured directly after nanoparticle preparation. Standard deviations are ±9.6 nm, ±0.011, ±3.3 mV, ±3.5%, and ±0.2% respectively.

notes: PLGA nanoparticles loaded with Oxcarbazepine. Formulation uses 0% w/v α-tocopherol polyethylene glycol-1000-succinate (TPGS) as surfactant. PLGA has inherent viscosity 0.15–0.25 dL/g.
evidence_span_text:
rain barrier (hCMEC/D3 cells) and human placental trophoblast
cells (BeWo b30 cells). Oxcarbazepine-loaded nanoparticles with encapsulation efficiency above
